description: Identify practical constraints on fiscal policy — recognition, legislative, and impact lags, political economy, and crowding-out effects — that limit its effectiveness

fields.name: lag_estimates
fields.label: Estimated recognition, action, and impact lags (months)

three_lags.recognition_lag: Time to detect that the economy needs stimulus or tightening
three_lags.action_lag: Time to pass legislation / execute policy
three_lags.impact_lag: Time for policy to transmit to aggregate demand

typical_duration.action_lag: Several months to 1-2 years depending on legislative process
typical_duration.impact_lag: 6-18 months for spending to feed through

political_economy.procyclical_temptation: Politicians favour stimulus in good times to attract votes
political_economy.structural_vs_cyclical_confusion: Temporary revenue booms misread as permanent

crowding_out.mechanism: Fiscal deficits -> higher interest rates -> lower private investment
crowding_out.open_economy: Higher rates attract capital -> currency appreciation -> net exports fall
crowding_out.limit_during_slack: Crowding out is minimal when slack is high and rates are pinned low

difficulties: Political constraints on raising taxes or cutting spending

applications.rates_trading: Anticipate Treasury issuance shifts from large fiscal plans
applications.equity_factor_exposure: Infrastructure spending benefits specific sectors with long impact lag
applications.currency: Pro-cyclical fiscal stance weakens currency via twin deficits

assess_implementation_risk.description: Rate lag and political risks of a fiscal proposal

high_implementation_risk.description: Long lags or political friction likely

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume announced fiscal stimulus equals actual stimulus
anti_patterns.why: Implementation lags, reappropriation, and political compromises often shrink headline numbers materially

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore crowding-out when rates are above the zero lower bound
anti_patterns.why: Above ZLB, deficits meaningfully lift rates; standard closed-economy multipliers overstate impact

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use historical lag estimates in a changed policy environment
anti_patterns.why: Digital disbursement, automatic stabilisers, and central bank financing have shortened some historical lags

anti_patterns.rule: Do not disregard the political business cycle in mature democracies
anti_patterns.why: Pre-election stimulus and post-election consolidation distort timing and magnitude of multipliers

lag_taxonomy.inside_lag: Recognition + action (time to decide and enact)
lag_taxonomy.outside_lag: Impact lag (time for effect to materialise)
